unlink_fileZ

o2net_timeoutZ

recycle_release
enable_io_debugc
EventKeeperHelpera
    Event keeper just works with kernel/ocfs2 for now.
    Event keeper has a strong relationship with Netlink communication channel,
    which is a way to receive and send Netlink messages between userland and
    kernel. Netlink dispatcher is abstraction layer for Netlink socket
    interfaces. So ocfs2 doesn't has to know anything about Netlink usage at
    all. Also, Event Keeper helper can record various events from or not from
    kernel persisting them into database or just particular files.
    c
targetTz%Thread identifier %s for saving eventr:
socketZ
gethostnameZ	host_name
nl_dispatcher
Queue
nl_event_queue
	threading
Thread
_watch_event_keeper
thread
	setDaemon
startr
info
ident
subscribe_event
netlink_event_handle
event_timestamp)
selfr?
__init__

zlz(|
        Handle messages from kernel/event keeper
        z
Start to watch event keeperT)
blockz
Received event %sz0Fail in receiving message from kernel reason: %sN)	r
record_event_locally

Z	task_done)
z%EventKeeperHelper._watch_event_keeperc
        Register to netlink event dispatcher for subscribling events.
        N)
put)
z&EventKeeperHelper.netlink_event_handlec
        Record these events into a separate log file for now.
        In the future, we should try to record them into database
        z	fsm.event

EventDispatcherz,Start a monitor to receive netlink messages.c

z*Thread identifier %s for dispatching event)
NetlinkSocket

NETLINK_PORT
nl_sock
	callbacksrB
_dispatch_eventrF
EventDispatcher.__init__
all_eventsc
        By register a callback to subscribe all
        events in default, also you can subscribe
        the specific event.
        Blocked on this callback, so we should make
        sure handle code does not waste any time.
        :param event: event name, default value all_event
                      is a particular event name that represent
                      subscribling all events
        :type event: string
        N)
callablerg
callbackZ
